title: Create HeroSection Component
description: Develop a new, self-contained HeroSection React component for the landing page introduction.
details:
- Create a new directory: src/components/landing/HeroSection/ - Implement HeroSection.tsx using semantic HTML (e.g.,
,

,

,
test_strategy: Write unit/component tests (React Testing Library) to verify rendering of headline, description, and CTA button. Check accessibility attributes and keyboard navigation.

title: Style HeroSection for Responsiveness and Visual Consistency
description: Apply responsive and visually consistent styles to the HeroSection component using CSS Modules or global styles.
details:
- Create HeroSection.module.css in the same directory for scoped styles, or extend src/app/globals.css if global changes are needed.
- Ensure the section is visually appealing and adapts to mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports.
- Use spacing, typography, and color schemes consistent with existing app design.
- Leverage utility classes or variables if present in the codebase.
test_strategy: Perform visual regression and manual UI tests across breakpoints. Use browser dev tools to verify responsiveness. Optionally, use automated visual regression tools.

title: Integrate HeroSection into Main Landing Page
description: Import and render the HeroSection component in src/app/page.tsx, ensuring it appears as the primary introduction on the landing page.
details:
- Modify src/app/page.tsx to import and display the HeroSection at the top of the page.
- Ensure the chat playground is either moved to a separate route (e.g., /playground) or conditionally rendered after the hero section, as appropriate for the current routing structure.
- Confirm that the hero section is the first thing users see on the root route ('/').
test_strategy: Write integration tests to confirm the HeroSection appears on the landing page and does not interfere with chat functionality. Manually verify correct placement and routing.

title: Implement CTA Button Functionality
description: Wire up the HeroSection's CTA button to start a new chat session or navigate to the chat playground, reusing existing logic or routes.
details:
- Use existing button components from src/components/ui/ if available for consistency.
- On click, navigate to the chat playground (e.g., using Next.js router to push '/playground') or trigger the new chat logic as implemented in the playground.
- Ensure the action is accessible and provides visual feedback (e.g., focus ring, loading state if needed).
test_strategy: Write integration tests to verify that clicking the CTA button initiates a new chat or navigates as intended. Manually test navigation and accessibility.
